one_line_description: CRM (formerly ProsperWorks) built around Google Workspace, managing leads, contacts, companies, opportunities, and pipelines directly from Gmail.
auth_summary: API keys are self-generated in the Copper web app (System settings > API Keys) and sent as X-PW-AccessToken + X-PW-UserEmail headers on every request. OAuth2 Authorization Code flow is supported for partner integrations, but registering an OAuth client requires emailing partners@copper.com — it is not self-serve.
access_notes: API keys are self-serve inside any Copper account, but the Web API is only available on trial, Professional, and Business plans (not Starter/Basic). A free 14-day trial grants API access; OAuth client_id/client_secret requires contacting Copper's partner team (partners@copper.com).
api_surface_summary: REST API (v1, JSON) at api.copper.com/developer_api/v1 covering leads (incl. bulk ops and conversion), people, companies, opportunities, projects, tasks, activities, tags, custom fields, field layouts, related items, file upload, users/account, and webhooks.
main_blocker: OAuth2 app registration is gated behind emailing partners@copper.com, and API access requires a Professional+ (or trial) plan; API-key auth itself is fully self-serve.
agent_notes: Fetched official developer docs directly. Copper's unusual header-pair auth (X-PW-AccessToken + X-PW-UserEmail) reflects its ProsperWorks legacy. No official Copper MCP found; multiple community/aggregator MCPs exist (Apideck, Pipedream, viaSocket, n8n, SyncHub). Rate limits not documented on the pages fetched. Note developer.copper.co (different TLD) is an unrelated crypto company — easy to confuse.
